arts.ttl: Arsenal injury news for Everton clash amid Gabriel update and Timber return
arts.sum: With that being said, football.london has taken a look at the latest Arsenal injury news as the Gunners are back at Emirates Stadium for a Premier League clash on Saturday. Six players missed the open training session at London Colney on Tuesday with Thomas Partey and Jurrien Timber as new absentees.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim breaks silence on Dan Ashworth's Man United exit amid Arsenal links
arts.sum: Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim has admitted that Dan Ashworth departing Old Trafford is a 'bad situation' amid reports that Arsenal could be interested in his services. Addressing the situation for the first time, Amorim insisted that the outgoing sporting director had always supported him despite claims from our colleagues at the Manchester Evening News that Ashworth was vying for Gareth Southgate to replace Erik ten Hag.

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Mikel Arteta drops Gabriel Jesus hint as forgotten man set for return
arts.sum: Arsenal are set to be active in the January transfer window as their Premier League title challenge continues to stall, with leaders Liverpool holding a commanding advantage

arts.ttl: Man City crisis takes fresh twist with Champions League nightmare handing Arsenal major boost
arts.sum: Were Man City to still be in the title race by then, they will have a huge challenge on their hands to keep pace with Arsenal, who after Wednesday's win are in a good position to reach the Round of 16 without having to feature in a Champions League play-off. However, Man City ended the night with only one win in their last 10 outings as Juventus ran out 2-0 winners.

arts.ttl: Every word from Mikel Arteta's post-Monaco presser
arts.sum: He tackled questions about the displays of Bukayo Saka and Myles Lewis-Skelly, taking our goalscoring chances and how we're settling into the rhythm of Champions League games. We need to go through certain moments, and I’m sure we will in the near future, but the fact that we can compete against any team that’s a positive thing to take, but understand how difficult it is to do it consistently in this competition.

arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka remains vital for Arsenal, but others need to step up
arts.sum: Both Gabriel Jesus and Oleksandr Zinchenko will need to raise their level at Arsenal if they will want to continue to be important for Mikel Arteta.

arts.ttl: Arsenal 'launch internal investigation into a member of staff' - after 'alleged antisemitic social media posts' were shared under his name
arts.sum: The user on social media site X (formerly Twitter ) is believed to have used a picture of Bonnick, and a variant of his name, although the outlet reports that the account was made private on Wednesday.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta heaps praise on Myles Lewis-Skelly after 18-year-old starred on first Champions League start - and suggests he could become a regular in injury-hit position
arts.sum: ISAAN KHAN AT THE EMIRATES: Mikel Arteta (right) hinted that Myles Lewis-Skelly (left) has a chance of making the vacant left-back spot his own after starring against Monaco.

arts.ttl: Thierry Henry makes his allegiances clear with Arsenal and Barcelona response
arts.sum: Two of Thierry Henry's former clubs, Arsenal and Monaco, played each other in the Champions League, while another of his ex-employers, Barcelona, came up against Borussia Dortmund

arts.ttl: Saka denied a first Arsenal hat trick by his own teammate in Champions League win
arts.sum: Still, with three goal contributions, Saka was again decisive for Arsenal and manager Mikel Arteta said the 23-year-old right winger was on his way to reaching a world-class level. “He got in the way of it,” Saka said about Havertz, laughing, “but don’t worry, it’s (a hat trick) coming.
